seo.title: RabbitMQ Daily Health Digest | Qarote
seo.description: Scheduled email reports summarising queue health, consumer counts, throughput trends, and DLQ accumulation — delivered to your inbox every morning.

hero.title: Your RabbitMQ health report, delivered every morning.
hero.subtitle: Qarote compiles queue depth trends, consumer counts, dead-letter accumulation, and message throughput into a single digest email. Know what happened overnight before you open your laptop.

items.title: Queue depth summary
items.description: The top queues by message count at report time, plus peak depth over the past 24 hours. Spot queues that filled overnight and drained before morning — the kind of silent near-miss that logs never capture.

items.title: Consumer health
items.description: Consumer count per queue at report time, flagging any queue that spent time with zero consumers. A queue that loses consumers for 10 minutes at 3am is an incident waiting to happen — the digest surfaces it before it repeats.

items.title: Dead-letter accumulation
items.description: DLQ message counts and growth rate over the past 24 hours. Dead-lettered messages indicate failed processing — a DLQ that quietly grew to 50,000 messages overnight is a backlog you want to know about before it becomes a replay incident.

items.title: Throughput trends
items.description: Publish and delivery rates charted over 24 hours. Throughput that dropped to zero at 2am and recovered by 4am shows up clearly in the digest — giving you the timeline for an incident you didn't even know happened.

items.title: Broker health snapshot
items.description: Memory usage, disk usage, and any active alarms across all connected brokers. A broker that hit its memory watermark briefly overnight and recovered is visible in the digest — before it happens again and takes your publishers down.

items.title: Top offenders
items.description: The queues with the highest depth, highest DLQ growth, and most consumer churn over the reporting window. Prioritised so the first section of the digest is always actionable — no scrolling through 200 healthy queues to find the two that need attention.

howItWorks.heading: Set it up once, receive it every morning

steps.title: Connect your broker
steps.description: Qarote collects queue metrics, consumer counts, and broker health data continuously via the RabbitMQ management API. No additional configuration is needed beyond your initial connection.

steps.title: Configure your schedule
steps.description: Choose your delivery time and time zone. Daily digests run in the morning by default. Add recipients — the digest goes to the whole team, not just the person who set it up.

steps.title: Receive your briefing
steps.description: A structured email arrives with your RabbitMQ health summary. Issues are surfaced first. Healthy queues are summarised. You spend two minutes reading it, not twenty minutes checking dashboards.

whyDigest.heading: Dashboards require intent. The digest requires nothing.
whyDigest.p1: A dashboard is reactive: you open it when you suspect a problem. The digest is proactive: it arrives whether or not you thought to check. Teams that run the digest consistently find incidents that would have gone undetected for hours — the kind that happen overnight, recover partially, and leave the queue in a degraded state by morning.
whyDigest.p2: The RabbitMQ management plugin retains metrics for roughly 8 hours. If something happened at 2am and you check the dashboard at 10am, the evidence is gone. The digest captures the 24-hour window and delivers it to you regardless of when you open your laptop.
whyDigest.p3: On-call rotations create coverage gaps. The digest fills them — every person on the team gets the same view of infrastructure health every morning, regardless of who was on call, who got paged, and what got resolved without a post-mortem.

items.question: Does the daily digest require a paid plan?
items.answer: The digest is a Developer and Enterprise plan feature. It requires historical metric data, which is part of Qarote's metrics persistence layer — available on paid plans. The free Community edition includes the real-time dashboard but not scheduled reporting.

items.question: Can I change the delivery schedule?
items.answer: Yes. You can configure the delivery time and time zone per workspace. Weekly digests are also available on Enterprise.

items.question: Who receives the digest?
items.answer: You configure the recipient list per workspace. Any team member with access to the workspace can be added as a recipient — useful for distributing visibility to engineers who don't log into the dashboard daily.

items.question: What time zone does the digest use?
items.answer: The digest uses the time zone configured in your workspace settings. Queue health data is always shown in UTC timestamps; the delivery schedule uses your local time zone.

items.question: Can I customise which queues appear in the digest?
items.answer: Yes. You can filter by vhost or queue name pattern to focus the digest on queues that matter to your team. Queues excluded from monitoring are excluded from the digest automatically.
